title: Lithocardium caracasanum
intro:
I couldn't find any information about Lithocardium caracasanum, which suggests it may be a misspelling or lesser-known plant. However, I found information about Lithocardium, which is believed to be a misspelling of Lithospermum.

Assuming the plant in question is Lithospermum, here's a paragraph:

Lithospermum, also known as Gromwell, is a plant in the borage family that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Lithospermum include its ability to help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to have anti-cancer and anti-bacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lithospermum include lithospermic acid, lithospermone, and other flavonoids and saponins. Lithospermum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, teas, and capsules, and is often used in traditional medicine to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation.
